exercises.title: 2-Minute Talk
exercises.description: Prepare and deliver a 2-minute talk on any topic. Record yourself on video. Watch it back and note: (1) What went well? (2) What to improve? (3) Filler words used?
hints: Choose a topic you know well
Focus on structure: hook → 3 points → close
Practice out loud at least 3 times before recording

solution.code:
Self-review checklist:
Opening: Did I have a strong hook? Y/N
Structure: Were my points clear? Y/N
Eye contact: Did I look at the camera/audience? Y/N
Pace: Too fast / Just right / Too slow
Volume: Too quiet / Just right / Too loud
Filler words (um, uh, like): Count ___
Body language: Open or closed?

Rating: ___/10
Top improvement for next time: ___

exercises.title: Storytelling Practice
exercises.description:
Tell a 3-minute story using this structure:
1. Character (who is it about?)
2. Conflict (what challenge did they face?)
3. Resolution (how was it resolved?)
4. Lesson (what's the takeaway?)
Tell it to a friend and ask for feedback.
hints: Show emotions — don't just tell us what happened

solution.code:
Story template:
Character: [Name] — [brief description]
Conflict: [What challenge/problem they faced]
Resolution: [How they overcame it]
Lesson: [What the audience should take away]

Practice telling it in under 3 minutes.

Feedback from listener:
- Was the story engaging? ___
- Was the lesson clear? ___
- What part was most memorable? ___
- What could be improved? ___

exercises.title: Impromptu Speaking
exercises.description: Set a timer for 2 minutes. Pick a random topic and speak about it for 2 minutes without stopping. Examples: 'Why breakfast is the most important meal,' 'The best advice I ever received,' 'Why we should have 4-day work weeks.'
hints: Use the 'PREP' structure: Point, Reason, Example, Point

solution.code:
PREP framework:
P - Point: State your main argument
R - Reason: Explain why
E - Example: Give a specific example
P - Point: Restate your argument

After each 2-minute round:
- Did I fill the full 2 minutes? Y/N
- Did I make sense? Y/N
- What was hardest? (starting / middle / ending)
- Confidence level: ___/10

Do 3 rounds with different topics.
